cwe: CWE-327 - Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm
CWE-347 - Improper Verification of Cryptographic Signature

threat.title: JWT Algorithm Confusion Attack (alg: none, RS256 to HS256)
threat.description: JWT algorithm confusion attacks exploit weak validation logic in JWT libraries where attackers manipulate the 'alg' header field to bypass signature verification. The two primary variants are: (1) Setting alg to 'none' to remove signature requirements entirely, and (2) Changing RS256 (asymmetric RSA) to HS256 (symmetric HMAC) to trick the server into using the public key as an HMAC secret. This allows attackers to forge arbitrary tokens and impersonate any user, including administrators, without knowing the signing key.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker intercepts a valid JWT token from their own authenticated session
2. Decodes the JWT header and payload (base64url encoded, not encrypted)
3. Modifies the 'alg' field in the header from 'RS256' to 'none' (or 'HS256')
4. Changes payload claims to impersonate admin user (e.g., userId: 1, role: 'admin')
5. For 'none' attack: Removes signature portion entirely, sends token as header.payload.
6. For RS256→HS256 attack: Obtains public key, uses it as HMAC secret to sign modified token
7. Sends forged token in Authorization: Bearer header to protected endpoints
8. Server's vulnerable JWT library accepts the manipulated token as valid
9. Attacker gains full administrative access to the application
prerequisites: JWT library does not enforce algorithm allowlisting
JWT library accepts 'none' algorithm without explicit rejection
For RS256→HS256: Public key is accessible (common in JWKS endpoints)
Server blindly trusts the 'alg' header field from untrusted tokens

cve_references.description: jsonwebtoken (Node.js) ≤4.2.1 - Algorithm confusion allowing RS256 tokens to be verified as HS256, enabling signature bypass
Cisco node-jose <0.11.0 - JWT key injection vulnerability allowing attackers to embed public keys in JWK header and forge tokens
Java ECDSA signature verification bypass ('Psychic Signatures') - Java 15-18 accepted blank signatures (r=0, s=0) for ECDSA-signed JWTs
Recent JWT algorithm confusion vulnerability where systems fail to properly verify the signature algorithm type

real_world_breaches.impact: Critical vulnerabilities found in multiple JWT libraries across languages (Node.js, Python, .NET) allowing 'alg: none' and RS256→HS256 attacks
real_world_breaches.root_cause: JWT libraries blindly trusted the 'alg' header without validation, following RFC 7519 too literally

real_world_breaches.impact: Authentication bypass in jitsi-meet video conferencing platform due to improper JWT validation
real_world_breaches.root_cause: JWT algorithm confusion allowing unauthorized meeting access

bug_bounty_reports.summary: Linktree Account Takeover via JWT validation bypass - Backend services did not properly validate JWTs, allowing expired token acceptance
Argo CD JWT audience claim vulnerability - Improper authorization allowing acceptance of invalid tokens

security_research.title: Critical Vulnerabilities in JSON Web Token Libraries
security_research.key_finding: Systematic analysis revealed that most JWT libraries accepted 'alg: none' tokens and were vulnerable to RS256→HS256 confusion attacks. Tim McLean's research led to RFC 8725 best practices.

security_research.title: Algorithm Confusion Attacks - PortSwigger Web Security Academy
security_research.key_finding: Comprehensive lab environment demonstrating JWT algorithm confusion attacks, showing real-world exploitation techniques for both 'none' and RS256→HS256 variants.

security_research.title: RFC 8725 - JSON Web Token Best Current Practices
security_research.key_finding: Official IETF standard explicitly recommending algorithm allowlisting and rejecting 'none' algorithm in response to Tim McLean's research findings.

mitigations.title: Implement Strict Algorithm Allowlisting
mitigations.description: Configure JWT verification to explicitly allowlist only the expected algorithm(s) and reject all others, including 'none'. This is the single most effective defense against algorithm confusion attacks. Modern JWT libraries provide algorithm validation options that should always be used.
nist_csf: PR.AC-7 - Users, devices, and other assets are authenticated
validation_steps: Review all JWT verification code to ensure 'algorithms' parameter is explicitly set
Test by sending token with 'alg: none' - should be rejected with 401/403
Test by sending RS256 token when HS256 expected - should be rejected
Verify algorithm mismatch attempts are logged for security monitoring
Use automated security testing tools (jwt_tool, Burp Suite JWT extensions)

mitigations.title: Enforce Strong Signature Verification with Key Pinning
mitigations.description: Implement robust signature verification that validates not only the signature itself but also binds the expected signing key to the specific algorithm. For asymmetric algorithms (RS256, ES256), always verify using the trusted public key retrieved from a secure source, never from the JWT header.
iso27001_controls: A.10.1.1 - Policy on the use of cryptographic controls
nist_csf: PR.AC-1 - Identities and credentials are issued and managed
validation_steps: Implement JWKS endpoint on authentication server
Configure JWKS caching with appropriate TTL (5-10 minutes)
Test that tokens with embedded JWK headers are rejected
Verify that only keys from trusted JWKS endpoint are accepted
Test key rotation scenario - ensure smooth transition
Monitor JWKS fetch failures and implement fallback mechanisms

mitigations.title: Harden JWT Library Configuration with Security Defaults
mitigations.description: Use modern JWT library versions with secure defaults and enable all available security features. Upgrade legacy libraries known to have algorithm confusion vulnerabilities and implement defense-in-depth through additional token validation checks.
nist_csf: PR.IP-2 - A System Development Life Cycle is implemented
DE.CM-1 - The network is monitored to detect anomalies
validation_steps: Audit all dependencies - ensure JWT libraries are latest versions
Check for CVE-2015-9235, CVE-2018-0114 in dependency scan
Implement automated tests for algorithm confusion attacks
Enable security logging for all JWT verification failures
Test token revocation mechanism with jti tracking
Perform penetration testing with jwt_tool and Burp Suite

indicators: JWT tokens with 'alg: none' appearing in authentication headers
Algorithm field mismatches (e.g., RS256 expected but HS256 received)
Sudden spike in JWT verification failures from same IP
Tokens with valid structure but invalid signatures
Admin-level access from user accounts with low privileges
Multiple JWT verification errors with different algorithms
Tokens with suspicious claims (future 'iat', invalid 'sub' format)
Access attempts using expired or revoked tokens

log_queries.query:
// Detect JWT algorithm anomalies and none algorithm usage
AppServiceHTTPLogs
| extend jwt_token = extract("Bearer ([A-Za-z0-9-_]+\\.[A-Za-z0-9-_]+\\.[A-Za-z0-9-_]*)", 1, CsReferer)
| where isnotempty(jwt_token)
| extend header_b64 = split(jwt_token, '.')[0]
| extend header_json = base64_decode_tostring(header_b64)
| extend alg = extract('"alg"\\s*:\\s*"([^"]+)"', 1, header_json)
| where alg in~ ('none', 'None', 'NONE') or alg !in~ ('HS256', 'RS256', 'ES256')
| summarize 
    AlgorithmAttempts = count(),
    UniqueTokens = dcount(jwt_token),
    Algorithms = make_set(alg),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
  by CIp, UserAgent
| where AlgorithmAttempts >= 3
| order by AlgorithmAttempts desc

index=app_logs sourcetype=express_access
| rex field=_raw "Bearer (?<jwt>[A-Za-z0-9-_]+\.[A-Za-z0-9-_]+\.[A-Za-z0-9-_]*)"
| rex field=jwt "^(?<header>[A-Za-z0-9-_]+)\."
| eval header_decoded = urldecode(replace(header, "-", "+"))
| eval header_decoded = replace(header_decoded, "_", "/")
| rex field=header_decoded "\"alg\":\"(?<algorithm>[^\"]+)\""
| where match(algorithm, "(?i)none") OR NOT match(algorithm, "(?i)(HS256|RS256|ES256)")
| stats 
    count as attempts,
    dc(jwt) as unique_tokens,
    values(algorithm) as algorithms,
    earliest(_time) as first_seen,
    latest(_time) as last_seen
  by client_ip, user_agent
| where attempts >= 3
| sort - attempts

// Detect JWT algorithm confusion attempts
event.category: "authentication" AND http.request.headers.authorization: *Bearer*
| eval jwt_parts = split(http.request.headers.authorization, ".")
| eval header_json = base64decode(jwt_parts[0])
| eval alg = json_extract(header_json, "$.alg")
| where alg IN ("none", "None", "NONE") OR alg NOT IN ("HS256", "RS256", "ES256", "HS384", "HS512")
| stats 
    count() as attempts,
    cardinality(http.request.id) as unique_requests,
    values(alg) as algorithms
  by source.ip, user_agent.original
| where attempts >= 3
| sort attempts desc

fields @timestamp, @message, httpRequest.clientIp, httpRequest.headers.authorization
| filter httpRequest.headers.authorization like /Bearer/
| parse httpRequest.headers.authorization /Bearer (?<jwt>[A-Za-z0-9-_]+\.[A-Za-z0-9-_]+\.[A-Za-z0-9-_]*)/
| parse jwt /^(?<header_b64>[A-Za-z0-9-_]+)\./
| fields @timestamp, httpRequest.clientIp, header_b64
// Note: CloudWatch doesn't have base64 decode, use Lambda for decoding
| stats count(*) as attempts by httpRequest.clientIp, header_b64
| filter attempts >= 3
| sort attempts desc

tools.name: jwt_tool
tools.description: Automated JWT attack tool testing for algorithm confusion, none attacks, and signature verification bypass

tools.name: Burp Suite JWT Editor Extension
tools.usage: Install JWT Editor extension in Burp Suite to manipulate JWT tokens and test algorithm confusion
tools.description: Manual JWT manipulation and testing for algorithm confusion vulnerabilities

tools.name: OWASP ZAP
tools.usage: Enable JWT Scan Rule in ZAP to detect JWT algorithm vulnerabilities
tools.description: Automated detection of JWT security issues including algorithm confusion
